introduction: This refers to tumours originating in the lung parenchyma or within the bronchi. Incidence is rising due to increase in smoking in both sexes, with nearly half of new cases diagnosed in the low income countries.
investigations: Radiologic: chest radiograph, contrast-enhanced chest CT, PET-CT
Others: FBC, Electrolyte, Urea and Creatinine, LFT and Serum proteins
prevention: Avoidance of smoking including secondhand smoke
Reducing occupational exposure to lung carcinogens
definitive_treatment: mainstay for stage I disease (wedge resection, segmentectomy, lobectomy, pneumonectomy, mediastinal node sampling)
Surgery + adjuvant chemotherapy: stages II and III disease
Palliative chemotherapy + radiotherapy: for stage IV disease
Targeted therapy: in selected cases (eg vascular endothelial growth factor inhibitor like bevacizumab)
